element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns car control in initial stage before competitor difficulty increases
Players can accelerate from 100 km/h to 255 km/h; mastery expected before mid-game
Collision with obstacles or vehicles causes flip/crash; increasing complexity through stages
Checkered flag markers add time; player must reach next checkpoint before time expires

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Stages progress from Volkswagen Beetles to Ferrari Testarossas
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Competitors get faster as stages progress; late-game features top-tier opposition
Eight distinct American locations (Key West, Big Apple, Gettysburg, Monument Valley, Las Vegas, Rocky Mountains, Twilight California, Bay Bridge); environmental variety provides visual narrative
Player can change background music mid-race (3 available tracks, though only 2 accessible in normal play)

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Accessible via Select button anytime during play
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional enhancement; toggleable without affecting core gameplay

typical_relief_triggers: Stage completion / transition to new environment
typical_tension_triggers: Approaching checkpoint deadline with low time remaining
Time running out causes vehicle to coast to stop (grace period of 5-10 seconds)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty curve with time-pressure segments creating pulse-based tension-relief cycles. Stage transitions provide reset points. Graceful timeout deceleration reduces sudden failure frustration.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: 3D stereoscopic mode toggle (Select button) - optional visual enhancement accessible without affecting progression
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden level select accessible via title screen (B button to increment level, Up+Left+Start to begin selected stage)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1-2 (Key West, Big Apple): Introduction to basic mechanics, slower initial competitors, manageable checkpoint timing
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stage 3-5 (Gettysburg, Monument Valley, Las Vegas Blvd): Increased competitor vehicle performance, tighter checkpoint windows, more complex traffic patterns
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 6-8 (Rocky Mountains, Twilight California, Bay Bridge): Fastest competitors (Ferrari Testarossas), maximum time pressure, full mastery of turbo and evasion required
notable_spikes: Transition from Stage 2 to Stage 3 marks shift from introductory to intermediate difficulty
Stage 6 onwards features Ferrari Testarossas as primary competition (significant spike)

notable_level_patterns.description: 8-stage sequence following West Coast to East Coast American geography (Key West, Big Apple, Gettysburg, Monument Valley, Las Vegas, Rocky Mountains, Twilight California, Bay Bridge). Each location provides distinct skyline and environmental visual theme serving as difficulty progression scaffold.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 1: Key West (coastal, warm palette) → Stage 8: Bay Bridge (metropolitan, cool palette)

notable_level_patterns.description: Vehicle opposition progresses from economy cars (Volkswagen Beetles) in early stages to luxury performance vehicles (Ferrari Testarossas) in late stages, creating emergent difficulty without explicit mechanic changes.

Each stage divides course into segments marked by checkered flag checkpoints. Passing checkpoint adds time; failure to reach before expiration ends race. Graceful 5-10 second coasting period mitigates sudden failure.

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Rad Racer II page)
wikipedia (Rad Racer / Rad Racer II article)
